title: light fermented algerian danmuji soup
description: a paleo-friendly soup that combines the tanginess of fermented ingredients with the creaminess of coconut milk and the crunch of danmuji. this dish is a unique twist on traditional algerian cuisine.

ingredients.name: coconut milk
ingredients.notes: full-fat coconut milk is essential for the creaminess of this dish.

ingredients.name: fermented cauliflower blend
ingredients.notes: use a store-bought or homemade fermented cauliflower blend for this recipe.

instructions: heat the coconut oil in a large saucepan over medium heat.
add the onion and garlic and cook until the onion is translucent, about 5 minutes.
add the cumin, coriander, salt, and black pepper, and cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly.
add the fermented cauliflower blend, water, and diced danmuji, and bring to a boil.
reduce the heat to low and simmer for 15-20 minutes, or until the flavors have melded together and the soup has thickened slightly.
stir in the coconut milk and cook for an additional 5 minutes.
taste and adjust the seasoning as needed.
ladle the soup into copper mugs and garnish with chopped fresh parsley.
